examples.title: Basic alarm() usage for timeout
examples.description: Demonstrates setting up an alarm for timeout on a blocking operation
examples.explanation: Sets up a 5-second timeout for the read() call. If read blocks for more than 5 seconds, SIGALRM is delivered, interrupting the read. alarm(0) cancels any remaining alarm time after the operation completes.

examples.title: Implementing sleep using alarm and pause
examples.description: Shows how sleep can be implemented using alarm() and pause()
examples.explanation: This implements a sleep function that avoids race conditions by using sigprocmask() to block SIGALRM during the critical section. The sigsuspend() temporarily unblocks the signal and pauses atomically. Returns remaining time if alarm was cancelled.

examples.title: setitimer() for periodic timer
examples.description: Demonstrates periodic timer using setitimer() with ITIMER_REAL
examples.explanation: This demonstrates periodic timer using setitimer(). The it_value field specifies when the first expiration occurs, and it_interval specifies the period for subsequent expirations. Setting both to zero disables the timer.

examples.title: Profiling with ITIMER_PROF
examples.description: Shows how to use ITIMER_PROF for performance profiling
examples.explanation: ITIMER_PROF counts time in both user and kernel mode. This is useful for profiling - the timer fires periodically while your code runs, allowing you to measure where CPU time is spent. The ticks indicate how much CPU time was used.
